{"key_event":"Elected local government and New Unionism expand","reasoning":"Elected county, parish, district, and Irish local councils, plus education and workplace regulation, increased administrative penetration and legal enforcement, while naval mobilization added coercive-fiscal capacity despite Irish legitimacy strains. Civil society also grew from an already very high base through New Unionism, early labour politics, women’s local participation, Irish tenant/local democracy, and mass education, though the Lords’ Home Rule veto and Parnell split limited the gain.","society_change":0.3,"society_power":9.4,"state_change":0.2,"state_power":9.3}
